text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: I didn't win? Are you kidding me?!
choice_data_list: You can aim for first next time!

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: I didn't win? Are you kidding me?!
choice_data_list: You can aim for first next time!

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: So what? It doesn't mean squat if I don't place!
choice_data_list:
You've just gotta play to your strengths, and
you'll be sure to win!

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text:
Isn't the whole reason I lost because I don't have
any strengths?
choice_data_list:
But you do! I can point them out to you while
we watch the replays together.

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text:
No thanks. That sounds like torture. I'll take the
recordings, though.

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text:
(I suppose watching my past races for reference
isn't such a bad idea.)

A few days later, I give Narita Taishin the replays
along with some notes on her running.

She seems annoyed by the notes, but thankfully,
she takes everything, and we get started on our
training session.

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: I'm going to win and win and win...

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text:
And I'll rise high above the others so that no
one'll ever talk bad about me!

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: Sigh... You're really getting too fired up about this.

The moment we get back, I look over her training
schedule and talk to her about the next race,
but...

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: I'll win the next one, so calm down already!

Despite being fed up with me, Narita Taishin is
dead set on claiming victory.
